sibling.name: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon
Sir John Fitz William De Barri

zh.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon是William de Barry的兒子，於大約1205年出生在威爾士。他至少有2個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon是William de Barry的儿子，于大约1205年出生在威尔士。他至少有2个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Money
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English moneye ‘money’ (Old French moneie, Latin moneta), perhaps a nickname for a rich man or for a moneyer. Compare Minter and Monier .
Irish (Derry): variant of   Mooney  .
Altered form of French   Monnet  .

variants.name: Minter
meanings: English (southeastern): occupational name for a moneyer, from Middle English myneter, mynter ‘moneyer’, an agent derivative of mynet ‘coin’, from Late Latin moneta ‘money’, originally an epithet of the goddess Juno (meaning ‘counselor’, from monere ‘advise’), at whose temple in Rome the coins were struck. The English term was used at an early date to denote a workman who stamped the coins; later it came to denote the supervisors of the mint, who were wealthy and socially elevated members of the merchant class, and who were made responsible for the quality of the coinage by having their names placed on the coins.

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

en.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon was born about 1205, in Wales as the son of William de Barry. He had at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon est né environ 1205, à Galles, fils de William de Barry. Il a eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon wurde um 1205 in Wales, als Sohn von William de Barry geboren. Er hatte mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon è nato il circa 1205 nel Galles come figlio di William de Barry. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannonは約1205年にウェールズでWilliam de Barryの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon(은)는 대략 1205년 웨일스에서 William de Barry의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon nasceu aproximadamente 1205, em Gales filho de William de Barry. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon родился около 1205 г. в местности Уэльс. Его отца звали William de Barry. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: Philip of the Money Barry in Inninshannon nació aproximadamente 1205, en Gales como hijo de William de Barry. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos.
